LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 - CHERYL DUNYE, ALEXANDRA JUHASZ AND JINGLETOWN FILMS' "MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF CROSS-COMPLAINT" IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. CCP 435.5 REQUIRES A MEET-AND-CONFER "IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE SUCH A MOTION MAY BE FILED. COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES NO SUCH MEET-AND-CONFER. COUNSEL SHALL COMPLY WITH CCP 435.5. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO IMMEDIATELY MEET AND CONFER AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS' RESPONSE TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY FEBRUARY 22, 2021. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 - PLAINTIFFS CHERYL DUNYE, ALEXANDRA JUHASZ, JINGLETON FILMS DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. CCP 435.5 REQUIRES A MEET-AND-CONFER "IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE SUCH A MOTION MAY BE FILED. COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES NO SUCH MEET-AND-CONFER. COUNSEL SHALL COMPLY WITH CCP 435.5. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO IMMEDIATELY MEET AND CONFER AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS' RESPONSE TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY FEBRUARY 22, 2021. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS CHERYL DUNYE, ALEXANDRA JUHASZ, AND JINGLETON FILMS' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cross-Defendants' demurrer to the causes of action brought against Dunye is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the first, second, and third causes of action, and overruled as to the fourth cause of action. Cross-Defendants' demurrer to the causes of action brought against Jingletown is sustained as to all four causes of action.

The demurrer by Dunye as to the first, second, and third causes of action for fraud is sustained. The four causes of action for fraud are all based on the premise that Smolowitz and Dunye entered into agreements with Smolowitz and his company 13th Gen to finance and coordinate the films in exchange for rights to the films. (Cross-Compl. pars. 153, 161, 169, 178.) The Cross-Complaint alleges that in all four cases, Dunye never intended to abide by her promises. (Id. at pars. 154, 162, 170, 179.) "'Promissory fraud' is a subspecies of the action for fraud and deceit. A promise to do something necessarily implies the intention to perform; hence, where a promise is made without such intention, there is an implied misrepresentation of fact that may be actionable fraud . . . An action for promissory fraud may lie where a defendant fraudulently induces the plaintiff to enter into a contract." (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 638.) "In California, fraud must be pled specifically; general and conclusory allegations do not suffice . . . This particularity requirement necessitates pleading facts which show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Id. at 645.) The first through third causes of action fail to meet this standard. The cross-complaint fails adequately to allege how, where, and by what means these representations were made, and the first cause of action also insufficiently alleges when these representations were made. The allegations here are less specific than those in Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 221, 234.

The demurrer by Dunye to the fourth cause of action for fraud related to The Doers Club is overruled. The cross-complaint sufficiently alleges that on December 7, 2017, Dunye "made a promise to SMOLOWITZ that if he and his company 13th GEN would finance and . . . the resulting film rights would be held by 13th GEN for all purposes," in agreeing to Smolowitz's offer, via email. (Cross-Compl., pars. 79-86, 178.)

The demurrer by Jingletown as to the first through fourth causes of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend. "A plaintiff's burden in asserting a fraud claim against a corporate employer is even greater. In such a case, the plaintiff must "allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written." (Lazar, 12 Cal.4th at 645.) The cross-complaint fails to adequately set forth these required allegations.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Crosscomplaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support And Declaration Of Jordan Susman
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS CHERYL DUNYE, ALEXANDRA JUHASZ, JINGLETON FILMS' Motion To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cross-Defendant's motion to strike the cross-complaint's request for attorney's fees is granted. "We start with the basic proposition that each party to a lawsuit must pay its own attorney fees except where a statute or contract provides otherwise. (Code Civ. Proc., sec.1021.)." (Dell Merk, Inc. v. Franzia (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 443, 450.) There is no statutory basis for attorney's fees here. Civil Code section 1709 allows for damages caused by reliance, and while Penal Code sec. 496(c) allows for attorney's fees, there is no allegations of receipt of stolen property. (See Express, LLC v. Fetish Group, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2006) 464 F.Supp.2d 965, 979 ["Therefore, this Court holds that Express' investigative expenses do not constitute damages for the purpose of its fraud cause of action."]; Switzer v. Wood (2019) 35 Cal.App.5th 116, 125-126 ["Section 496(a) defines the criminal offense of what is commonly referred to as receiving stolen property, while section 496(c) sets forth a right to special civil remedies where a violation of section 496(a) has occurred."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Cheryl Dunye, Alexandra Juhasz and Jingletown Films' "motion to strike portions of cross-complaint" is ordered off calendar.
